Green auroral curtains ripple and fold across the polar night, casting ghostly light over silent tundra and frozen lakes below.
Massive supercell thunderstorm rotates slowly, its anvil top spreading flat against the stratosphere above empty grassland at dusk.
Mammatus clouds hang in gray pouches beneath the storm base, bulging downward like cellular structures over the darkened plains.
Lenticular cloud hovers motionless above the mountain summit, its smooth lens shape glowing pink in alpenglow at dawn.
Parallel rows of altocumulus wave clouds stretch across the entire sky, forming geometric ripples over the desert basin.
Milky Way arches from horizon to horizon, its dense stellar river visible above the dark silhouette of forest ridgeline.
Lightning fork branches across the night sky in fractal brilliance, illuminating thunderheads towering above the coastal marsh.
Golden god-rays pierce through storm cloud gaps, casting defined shafts of light onto distant hills and valley fog.
Bright sun-dogs flank the winter sun like false suns, refracting through ice crystals suspended above snow-covered fields.
Noctilucent clouds glow electric blue at twilight's edge, their wispy tendrils visible only after sunset over northern horizons.
Violet and crimson aurora australis undulates across the Antarctic sky, ribbons of light twisting above the glacial ice shelf.
Double rainbow arcs span the valley after the storm, vivid primary and faint secondary bands bridging forested mountain slopes.
Towering pyrocumulonimbus erupts from volcanic vent, its ash-laden thundercloud boiling upward into the stratosphere above barren lava fields.
Lunar halo encircles the full moon in perfect ring, ice crystals refracting light twenty-two degrees overhead the sleeping prairie.
Iridescent nacreous clouds shimmer with pearl and pastel rainbow sheens, rare stratospheric display glowing above polar twilight.
Crepuscular rays fan outward from sunset clouds in radiant spokes, golden beams stretching across the dimming desert horizon.
Monsoon rain curtain advances as visible gray wall across distant plains, sky-filling deluge connecting cloud base to earth.
Fog-bow forms pale white arc through morning mist, ghostly spectrum hovering above the dew-soaked moorland.
Ribbon lightning ripples horizontally between storm cells, glowing channels flickering across the night sky above dark ocean.
Sandstorm wall rises ochre and massive, churning dust column thousands of feet high engulfing the sky above flat steppe.
Circumzenithal arc blazes upside-down rainbow directly overhead, ice-crystal refraction painting vivid spectrum against deep blue zenith above winter taiga.
Kelvin-Helmholtz wave clouds curl in repeating spiral breakers, atmospheric turbulence sculpting the sky into ocean-like formations above prairie.
Virga curtains hang gray and wispy beneath high clouds, evaporating rain never reaching ground as visible streaks dissolve mid-sky over basin.
Polar stratospheric clouds glow iridescent orange and green at extreme altitude, rare mother-of-pearl sheen illuminating Arctic dawn sky.
Asperitas clouds undulate in turbulent waves overhead, chaotic pouched formations rippling darkly above forested hills before storm arrival.
Anticrepuscular rays converge toward eastern horizon at sunset, parallel shadow-bands appearing to meet at antisolar point above distant mountains.
Brocken specter projects observer's shadow onto fog bank crowned by circular glory rings, rare optical phenomenon above mist-filled crater.
Castellanus clouds tower in turrets along horizontal layer, vertical columns rising from stratiform deck signaling atmospheric instability overhead.
Fire rainbow circumhorizontal arc blazes parallel to horizon, rare ice-crystal alignment painting sky with flame-like spectrum above summer plateau.
Fallstreak hole opens circular void through cloud deck, descending ice crystals carving dramatic aperture revealing blue sky beyond.
Nacreous clouds shimmer pink and turquoise in stratospheric heights, mother-of-pearl bands glowing before sunrise above frozen Norwegian fjords.
Green and magenta auroral corona radiates directly overhead, curtains converging at magnetic zenith above Arctic spruce forest.
Shelf cloud advances dark and horizontal, massive arcus formation marking gust front rolling over wheat fields ahead of squall.
Tangent arcs flare upward from sun-dog points, rare halo complex blazing through ice fog suspended above Siberian tundra.
Pileus cap cloud forms smooth dome atop erupting thunderhead, transient silk veil stretched over boiling cumulus tower above plains.
Volcanic lightning crackles through ash plume, dirty thunderstorm illuminating pyroclastic column rising black against twilight sky.
Roll cloud rotates along horizontal axis, solitary tubular formation spinning slowly forward above Texas coastal wetlands at dawn.
Ice crystal pillars extend vertical shafts from setting sun, rare light pillar phenomenon beaming upward through diamond dust above lake.
Sprites flash red tendrils above distant thunderstorm tops, mesospheric electrical discharge reaching toward space from nocturnal supercell.
Wall cloud rotates beneath supercell base, lowering funnel condensation swirling dark against green-lit storm above Kansas grassland.
Blood-red solar pillar shoots vertically from horizon at Arctic sunset, ice crystals forming luminous column thousands of feet skyward above tundra.
Undulatus asperatus roils overhead in apocalyptic turbulence, chaotic underbelly churning like inverted ocean swells above canyon before deluge.
Tangent arc halo complex blooms around zenith sun, stacked crystalline refractions painting geometric rings through frigid diamond-dust fog overhead.
Anvil crawler lightning ripples horizontally across thunderhead top, glowing channels sprawling miles through stratospheric ice-cloud deck at nightfall.
Morning glory cloud rolls as tubular wave across gulf sky, thousand-foot cylinder rotating forward over coastal mudflats at dawn.
Parry arc curves upward from winter sun in rare convex bow, atmospheric optics bending light through tumbling plate crystals above snowfield.
Cumulonimbus incus spreads vast anvil shadow across afternoon sky, flattened storm crown blocking sun over hundred-mile radius above savanna.
Circumscribed halo encircles sun in elliptical loop, complex ice-crystal geometry projecting rare oval ring overhead frozen Alaskan wilderness.
Volcanic ash veil diffuses sunset into layered crimson bands, stratospheric aerosol scattering light across hemispheric sky above distant eruption.
Ice fog sun-dog projects twin vertical pillars flanking horizon orb, frozen prisms creating cruciform light geometry above glacier basin.
